tasks.title: Split internal/exporter/exporter.go into focused format modules
evidence: File is 835 LOC, exceeding the 700 LOC threshold
Contains implementations for multiple formats: JSON, JSONL, Markdown, CSV
Each format implementation has its own stream/buffer logic that could be co-located
Large file reduces maintainability and makes it hard to add new formats
plan: Analyze exporter.go to identify format-specific functions
Create separate format files: internal/exporter/json.go, jsonl.go, markdown.go, csv.go
Move format-specific types (ScrapeResult, CrawlResult, ResearchResult) to appropriate files or types.go
Keep Export and ExportStream entry points in exporter.go or move to a dispatcher file
Ensure each format file is cohesive and focused
Update all imports in tests and other packages
Run make test to verify tests pass
Run make ci to ensure full pipeline works

tasks.title: Review and standardize error handling patterns
evidence: Error handling patterns vary across packages: some use fmt.Errorf with context, others use errors.New
Validation errors in validate/request.go use Validate() methods on structs
Scheduler uses fmt.Errorf with detailed context (e.g., 'failed to resolve auth for scrape schedule %s: %w')
API server uses ErrorResponse struct for HTTP errors
Inconsistent error handling makes debugging harder and reduces predictability
plan: Search for all error return statements across internal/ to categorize patterns
Identify common error types: validation errors, not found errors, permission errors, internal errors
Create internal/errors/package or add to existing error patterns
Standardize on wrapping with context using fmt.Errorf('%s: %w', context, err)
Define error constructors for common patterns: ValidationError, NotFoundError, PermissionError
Update inconsistent error sites to use standardized patterns
Ensure error messages include enough context for debugging without exposing secrets
Run make test to verify all tests still pass
Run make ci to ensure full pipeline works

tasks.title: Review and standardize logging levels and messages
evidence: Logging uses slog consistently but level choices vary: crawl.go has 24 slog calls (mix of Debug, Info, Error, Warn)
adaptive_fetcher.go has 14 slog calls with detailed context about fetch decisions
Inconsistent use of structured fields: some logs include all context, others minimal
Debug logs for sensitive operations (e.g., auth handling) may expose secrets
plan: Review all slog.Error, Warn, Info, Debug calls across internal/
Categorize log levels: Error (failures requiring action), Warn (issues but continuing), Info (significant state changes), Debug (detailed flow)
Check for any logs that might expose secrets (auth tokens, passwords, API keys)
Redact or remove sensitive information from logs
Standardize on structured logging with consistent field names: url, jobID, component, duration
Document logging conventions in AGENTS.md or internal/README.md
Add tests that verify sensitive data is not logged in error paths
Run make test to verify tests pass
Run make ci to ensure full pipeline works

Ensure MCP tool handlers use shared validators

tasks.title: Verify generated TypeScript API types match Go API contracts

Type mismatches cause runtime errors in web client

Verify all API endpoints and types are correctly represented in OpenAPI spec

Consider adding 'make verify-api' target to check type consistency
Run make generate to ensure regeneration works
Run make ci to ensure full pipeline works

tasks.title: Review config.Load() singleton pattern for thread safety
evidence: config.Load() is called once in cli.go and config object is passed around
Search found only 1 call to config.Load() in the entire codebase
No evidence of concurrent access issues, but config could be accessed from multiple goroutines (scheduler, manager)
Config object should be immutable or thread-safe if shared across goroutines
plan: Read internal/config/config.go to understand Config struct and its fields
Check if Config struct is modified after Load() returns
Trace config usage across scheduler, manager, api to identify write patterns
If config is modified after initialization, make Config immutable (read-only fields) or add mutex
Document config access patterns in AGENTS.md or config.go package docs
Add tests that verify config thread safety if mutations are possible
Run make test to verify tests pass
Run make ci to ensure full pipeline works
